all_findings.issue: hooks/tests/INDEX.md appears in git diff --name-only since baseline but was not declared in dev.files_modified. The change is a single-line timestamp auto-update generated by posttool-doc-sync hook, not a dev-authored code change.
all_findings.recommendation: Dev should declare hook-auto-generated INDEX files in files_modified or the provenance system should exempt recognised doc-sync hook artifacts.

qa.notes: Fix confirmed at hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py:189-190. Both env vars ('CLAUDE_SESSION_ID', 'CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID') are in the strip tuple. AC1 1 passed in 0.02s; AC2 15 passed in 0.04s. Codex independently reproduced both results. One minor provenance note: hooks/tests/INDEX.md modified by doc-sync hook is not declared in dev.files_modified — non-blocking, hook-generated timestamp only.

spec_section_updates.section_4: hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py:189-190 — env dict comprehension now filters both CLAUDE_SESSION_ID and CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID. pytest AC1: 1 passed in 0.02s. pytest AC2: 15 passed in 0.04s (0 failed). hooks/tests/INDEX.md updated by doc-sync hook (timestamp only, not a code change).

out_of_scope_observations.observation: A generated test for a different task (20260524-122947, execute-push.py AC3) also strips only CLAUDE_SESSION_ID from the environment, mirroring the same env-var strip omission pattern as the bug fixed in this cycle. Per the BA-QA report, the test is already failing for an unrelated reason. This pattern may warrant a separate audit cycle for generated tests with the same class of env-var strip omission.
